title: ContractGuard assembly contract
description: Prescribes the API surface of one assembly. The stored form is always decomposed signature elements - C# declaration strings and golden DLLs are authoring inputs that tooling decomposes into this form. Semantics-affecting configuration lives in this file (not in CLI flags) so that contract changes and policy changes pass through the same review gate.

$schema.description: Reference to this schema, enabling editor validation and completion.

assembly.description: Simple name of the assembly this contract governs (no path, no .dll extension), e.g. 'MyCompany.Orders'. The gate matches it against the scanned assembly's name.

version.description: Optional shop-managed version label for this contract. Not interpreted by the gate.

description.description: Optional human description of this contract's intent.

usings.description: Namespaces used to resolve shorthand type names, in addition to each governed type's own namespace and built-in aliases (int, string, ...).

types.description: The governed types. Types not listed here are ungoverned (subject only to settings.newTypes).

significance.description: Whether the comparer treats this aspect as part of the contract.

mode.description: 'required': the member must exist exactly as prescribed. 'forbidden': the exact signature given must NOT exist (e.g. forbid a public parameterless constructor).

reason.description: Optional rationale, surfaced verbatim in gate diagnostics when this entry is violated.

typeName.description: A type reference written as a canonical name string - the one deliberate exception to element decomposition. Accepts C#-style shorthand resolved via 'usings' plus the governed type's namespace: generics (Task<Result>), arrays (int[]), nullable (string?), tuples ((int x, int y)). Nullable annotations on reference types are compared only when settings.nullableAnnotations is 'significant'.

attributeList.description: Attribute type names (the 'Attribute' suffix may be omitted).

variance.description: Variance of this type parameter. Interfaces and delegates only.

constraints.description: Constraint list: 'class', 'struct', 'notnull', 'unmanaged', 'new()', or base/interface type names.

paramPair.description: Compact [type, name] shorthand for a plain parameter. Anything with a modifier or default value uses the object form.

name.description: Parameter name. May be omitted when parameter names are not significant.

modifier.description: Parameter passing modifier. 'this' marks an extension-method receiver.

default.description: Presence of this property marks the parameter optional.

defaultValue.description: A constant as a typed JSON value. Enum constants may be written as the underlying number or as an 'EnumType.Member' string (the parameter's type disambiguates). {"$special": "default"} represents default(T) for value types with no representable constant; "NaN"/"Infinity"/"-Infinity" carry non-finite floating-point constants, which have no JSON number literal.

accessors.description: Accessor shape, each present accessor mapped to its accessibility (supports asymmetric cases like { get; private set; }). 'set' and 'init' are mutually exclusive. Omit the whole object to prescribe only the property's existence, type, and accessibility, leaving accessor shape ungoverned.

explicitInterface.description: The interface this member explicitly implements. Explicit implementations carry no accessibility of their own.

modifiers.description: Signature-level modifiers. 'async' is deliberately absent: it is an implementation detail, not part of the binary signature, and implementers may freely choose it.

params.description: Required even when empty ([]): the parameter list is a constructor's identity. Static constructors are not a contract concept.

explicitInterface.description: The interface this member explicitly implements. Explicit implementations carry no accessibility of their own.
The interface this member explicitly implements. Explicit implementations carry no accessibility of their own.
The interface this member explicitly implements. Explicit implementations carry no accessibility of their own.

value.description: For const fields: the constant value. Part of the contract - consumers compile the value in. Also used for enum members.

name.description: The operator symbol or conversion keyword: + - * / % == != < > <= >= & | ^ ! ~ ++ -- << >> true false, or 'implicit' / 'explicit' for conversions. Operators are always public static, so no access or modifiers are accepted here.

type.description: Full type name including namespace, e.g. 'MyCompany.Orders.OrderService'. Nested types use '+': 'Outer+Inner'.

access.description: The type's accessibility; defaults to public. A public type silently becoming internal is a violation.

attributes.description: Attribute type names prescribed on this type, compared only against settings.significantAttributes.

kind.description: When present, the type's kind is part of the contract (a class silently becoming a struct is a violation). Omit to govern members only.

underlyingType.description: Enum types only: the underlying integral type.

implements.description: Interfaces the type must implement. Not exhaustive: implementing additional interfaces is not a violation.

newMembers.description: May developers add members beyond those prescribed? Overrides settings.newMembers for this type.

parameterNames.description: Overrides settings.parameterNames for this type's members.

returns.description: Delegate types only: the delegate's return type.

params.description: Delegate types only: the delegate's parameter list.

settings.description: Contract-wide policy. Lives inside the contract file deliberately: CLI flags and MSBuild properties control only operational concerns and can never weaken these semantics.

newTypes.description: May developers add new types (within scanned scope) beyond those governed here?

newMembers.description: Default for whether governed types may gain members beyond those prescribed. Overridable per type.

parameterNames.description: Whether parameter names are part of the contract. Overridable per type and per member.

defaultValues.description: Whether optional-parameter default values are part of the contract. Defaults compile into call sites, so silent changes alter caller behavior.

nullableAnnotations.description: Whether nullable reference annotations (string vs string?) are part of the contract, decoded from NullableAttribute/NullableContextAttribute. Oblivious (pre-nullable) assemblies carry no annotations and compare as unannotated. Nullable value types (int?) are distinct types and always significant.

tupleElementNames.description: Whether tuple element names ((int x, int y) vs (int a, int b)) are part of the contract. Renames break source compatibility for consumers using named access.

scope.description: Accessibility levels the newTypes/newMembers deny sweeps treat as surface, and that 'extract' pulls out. Prescribed members are enforced regardless of their accessibility. 'protected internal' members are in scope if either level is listed.

significantAttributes.description: Attribute types the comparer pays attention to. Attributes not listed are invisible to the gate.
